name: Cheesy Ham and Corn Pie
recipeIngredient: 1/4 teaspoon pepper and salt to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Sauté onion and green pepper in butter in a large skillet until soft; remove from heat.
2. Beat eggs and milk in a bowl until blended. Add the onion, peppers mixture, ham, corn, cheese, Worcestershire sauce, and seasoning; mix well.
3. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
4. Prepare pie dough as desired (homemade or store-bought). Roll out slightly more than half for the bottom crust and place in a 11x17 inch baking dish. Spoon in the ham mixture.
5. Roll out remaining dough and fit over filling. Pinch together edges; crimp or flute. Cut steam vents on top.
6. Place on a cookie sheet or foil. Bake in the preheated oven for 1 hour, or until pastry is golden brown. Let stand a few minutes before cutting into squares.
Freezing Instructions:
- Assemble unbaked pies on a large cookie sheet; freeze until firm. Wrap tightly and label with date before storing in the freezer.
- To bake frozen pie: Remove foil from pies and place them on a large baking sheet. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 35 minutes, or until golden brown.
